mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the equity risk premium and discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic expansion, which increases corporate earnings visibility and lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation, supply constraints captured by the industrial production index become the dominant pricing factor, causing S&P 500 forward returns to decline as input costs compress corporate margins.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive or neutral in over 50% of high-inflation episodes despite declining INDPRO, or the negative predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth indicates expanding aggregate demand, which directly supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 20-day forward return correlation with GDPC1 growth rates remains below 0.10 over any 3-year window, or the signal produces negative returns in more than 55% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to compress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of trading days following FEDFUNDS crossings above 2.5%, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between payroll employment growth and the unemployment rate signals labor market inefficiencies that precede earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days when PAYEMS rises but UNRATE fails to decline.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the joint effect disappears when controlling for CPIAUCSL momentum.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, housing market activity captured by HOUST becomes a leading indicator of consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly positive when HOUST rises under FEDFUNDS below 0.5%, or the predictive power reverses sign when FEDFUNDS crosses above 1.0%.
